evidence: Covers data->tokenisation->quality/dedup->mixing->objectives->scaling->parallelism->mixed-precision->PEFT->instruction tuning->RLHF->DPO->domain adaptation->finance models->evaluation->bias->hallucination->governance.

Caveats present: look-ahead bias (rem:temporal-alignment), catastrophic forgetting / adaptation tax, Chinchilla data-boundedness for finance corpora (:1113-1121), perplexity limitations (:1253-1261), ROUGE recall-bias (:1303-1308).

notes: Audit only, no edits. Two BLOCKER duplicate-label collisions drag notation_crossref to 62: def:mlm (ch01:1786 vs ch03:389) and def:lora+eq:lora (ch02:2184 vs ch03:781). The ch02/ch03 LoRA definitions also disagree in formula (W0+BA vs W0+(alpha/r)BA), which additionally pulls down correctness (88) and non_repetition (86). code_figure_correctness (87) and reproducibility (80) are held back by a placeholder demo.ipynb and unexecuted live-EDGAR exercise cells; the illustration figure itself is reproducible and matches the caption exactly. citation_accuracy capped at 89 (NEEDS_EXTERNAL_VERIFICATION on headline numeric figures). All 33 cite keys resolve uniquely (citation_hygiene 95). Strong finance orientation, examples, completeness, and pedagogy. This chapter is the confirmed SSOT for scaling, PEFT, RLHF/DPO, and evaluation metrics.
